description: This figure presents a 12-by-12 correlation scatter matrix (pair plot) visualizing the multivariate relationships among eleven compositional variables—C, Si, Mn, Cr, S, Ni, P, Fe, Mo, V, and W (all in wt.%)—and Vickers microhardness (HV) for a dataset of Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steels used in machine learning model training. The diagonal panels display kernel density estimate histograms revealing the distribution range and sampling frequency of each individual variable, whereas the off-diagonal panels contain scatter plots that expose pairwise linear or nonlinear correlations between elemental contents and the hardness response. Visually, microhardness shows a pronounced negative correlation with Fe content and strong positive correlations with C, Cr, Ni, Mo, V, and W, trends that align with solid-solution strengthening mechanisms. Additionally, certain element pairs such as Cr-Fe and Mo-Fe form characteristic inverse clusters. This exploratory visualization provides the foundational statistical evidence for feature importance ranking and supports the subsequent SVR-based microhardness prediction and firefly-algorithm-driven inverse alloy design.
